title: Troubleshooting:
source_marker: Interpersonal Effectiveness Handout 9   (I. E. Worksheet 7) (p. 1 of 2)

pages.text:
134
From DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition by Marsha M. Linehan. Copyright 2015 by Marsha M. Linehan. Permission
to photocopy this handout is granted to purchasers of this book for personal use only (see copyright page for details).
Interpersonal Effectiveness Handout 9   (I. E. Worksheet 7) (p. 1 of 2)
Troubleshooting:
When What You Are Doing Isn’t Working
1
Do I have the skills I need? Check out the instructions.
Review what has already been tried.
•• Do I know how to be skillful in getting what I want?
•• Do I know how to say what I want to say?
•• Do I follow the skill instructions to the letter?
2
Do I know what I really want in this interaction?
Ask:
•• Am I undecided about what I really want in this interaction?
•• Am I unsure of my priorities?
•• Am I having trouble balancing:
•• Asking for too much versus too little?
•• Saying no to everything versus saying yes to everything?
•• Is fear or shame getting in the way of knowing what I really want?
3
Are short-term goals getting in the way of long-term goals?
Ask:
•• Is “now, now, now” winning out over getting what I really want in the
future?
•• Is emotion mind controlling what I say and do instead of Wise Mind?
(continued on next page)

135
Interpersonal Effectiveness Handout 9   (p. 2 of 2)
4
Are my emotions getting in the way of using my skills?
Ask:
•• Do I get too upset to use my skills?
•• Are my emotions so high that I am over my skills breakdown point?
5
Are worries, assumptions, and myths getting in my way?
Ask:
•• Are thoughts about bad consequences blocking my action?
“They won’t like me,” “She will think I am stupid.”
•• Are thoughts about not deserving things getting in my way?
“I am such a bad person I don’t deserve this.”
•• Am I calling myself names that stop me from doing anything?
“I won’t do it right,” “I’ll probably fall apart,” “I’m so stupid.”
•• Do I believe myths about interpersonal effectiveness?
“If I make a request, this will show that I am a weak person,”
“Only wimps have values.”
6
Is the environment more powerful than my skills?
Ask:
•• Are the people who have what I want or need more powerful than I am?
•• Are other people more in control of the situation than I am?
•• Will others be threatened if I get what I want?
•• Do others have reasons for not liking me if I get what I want?
7
Other ideas:

full_text:
Interpersonal Effectiveness Handout 9   (I. E. Worksheet 7) (p. 1 of 2)
Troubleshooting:
When What You Are Doing Isn’t Working
Do I have the skills I need? Check out the instructions.
Review what has already been tried.
•• Do I know how to be skillful in getting what I want?
•• Do I know how to say what I want to say?
•• Do I follow the skill instructions to the letter?
Do I know what I really want in this interaction?
Ask:
•• Am I undecided about what I really want in this interaction?
•• Am I unsure of my priorities?
•• Am I having trouble balancing:
•• Asking for too much versus too little?
•• Saying no to everything versus saying yes to everything?
•• Is fear or shame getting in the way of knowing what I really want?
Are short-term goals getting in the way of long-term goals?
Ask:
•• Is “now, now, now” winning out over getting what I really want in the
future?
•• Is emotion mind controlling what I say and do instead of Wise Mind?
(continued on next page)

Interpersonal Effectiveness Handout 9   (p. 2 of 2)
Are my emotions getting in the way of using my skills?
Ask:
•• Do I get too upset to use my skills?
•• Are my emotions so high that I am over my skills breakdown point?
Are worries, assumptions, and myths getting in my way?
Ask:
•• Are thoughts about bad consequences blocking my action?
“They won’t like me,” “She will think I am stupid.”
•• Are thoughts about not deserving things getting in my way?
“I am such a bad person I don’t deserve this.”
•• Am I calling myself names that stop me from doing anything?
“I won’t do it right,” “I’ll probably fall apart,” “I’m so stupid.”
•• Do I believe myths about interpersonal effectiveness?
“If I make a request, this will show that I am a weak person,”
“Only wimps have values.”
Is the environment more powerful than my skills?
Ask:
•• Are the people who have what I want or need more powerful than I am?
•• Are other people more in control of the situation than I am?
•• Will others be threatened if I get what I want?
•• Do others have reasons for not liking me if I get what I want?

ourspace_shared_life_filter.title: Troubleshooting:
ourspace_shared_life_filter.shared_facing_summary: Use this to protect both people: needs are real, boundaries are kind, and a pause is not abandonment when repair is named.
tiny_steps: Name the ask in one sentence.
Agree on a return time or next check-in.

flare_day_versions.minimum: One breath, one label, one safe message, or one checkbox.
flare_day_versions.medium: Two to five minutes with everything reachable from bed/chair.
flare_day_versions.full: Use the original worksheet/handout when both bodies and brains have capacity.

accessibility_modifications: Use text/chat if speech is hard.
Include caregiver capacity as part of the plan, not an afterthought.
relationship_and_caregiver_notes: William's needs and Jasper's capacity both count.
Ask before helping; ask before escalating; name the smallest next step.
Pause means pause, not abandonment. I am coming back. I care about us. I need a smaller next step.
What is needed, what capacity exists, what can be smaller, and what can wait?
identity_and_trauma_filter: Use William's masculine language and Jasper's they/them language.
Use consent-based, non-shaming, body-neutral language unless a person has explicitly chosen a term.
Do not turn symptoms, dysphoria, trauma responses, clutter, fatigue, or emotional intensity into moral failure.
avoid_or_adjust: Do not use skills to win, corner, threaten leaving, or make either person prove pain or disability.
